day.simpleMeaning: The time when the sun is up
day.contextMeaning: The time before night, when light was fading
day.sentence: The fox pawed the grass gently, and the stone sparkled in the last light of the day.

picked.simpleMeaning: Took something up with your hand
picked.contextMeaning: Took it up from the ground
picked.sentence: The traveler picked it up and held all three stones together in one hand.

held.contextMeaning: Kept the stones in one hand
held.sentence: The traveler picked it up and held all three stones together in one hand.

all.contextMeaning: The three stones together, not just one
all.sentence: The traveler picked it up and held all three stones together in one hand.

hand.simpleMeaning: The part at the end of your arm
hand.contextMeaning: The traveler used this body part to hold the stones
hand.sentence: The traveler picked it up and held all three stones together in one hand.

back.simpleMeaning: To the place you came from
back.sentence: Now they needed to go back to the bridge before it got too dark.

before.contextMeaning: They must return first, then it gets dark
before.sentence: Now they needed to go back to the bridge before it got too dark.

got.sentence: Now they needed to go back to the bridge before it got too dark.

voices.sentence: On the way back, they heard voices.

children.contextMeaning: Two young people stood near the trees
children.sentence: Two children stood near the trees, looking worried.

trees.simpleMeaning: Tall plants with wood and leaves
trees.contextMeaning: The big plants near the children
trees.sentence: Two children stood near the trees, looking worried.

looking.contextMeaning: They seemed worried when you saw them
looking.sentence: Two children stood near the trees, looking worried.

worried.simpleMeaning: Feeling scared something bad may happen
worried.sentence: Two children stood near the trees, looking worried.

boy.contextMeaning: The young male child who held his sister's hand
boy.sentence: The boy held his sister’s hand, and both of them looked scared.

sister.sentence: The boy held his sister’s hand, and both of them looked scared.

both.contextMeaning: The boy and his sister together looked scared
both.sentence: The boy held his sister’s hand, and both of them looked scared.

scared.sentence: The boy held his sister’s hand, and both of them looked scared.

come.contextMeaning: They went to the festival lights
come.sentence: They had come to see the festival lights, but they had walked too far and could not find the village again.

see.contextMeaning: They wanted to look at the festival lights
see.sentence: They had come to see the festival lights, but they had walked too far and could not find the village again.

far.contextMeaning: They walked a long way and got lost
far.sentence: They had come to see the festival lights, but they had walked too far and could not find the village again.

find.contextMeaning: They could not get back to the village
find.sentence: They had come to see the festival lights, but they had walked too far and could not find the village again.

again.contextMeaning: They could not find the village one more time
again.sentence: They had come to see the festival lights, but they had walked too far and could not find the village again.

cannot.contextMeaning: They are not able to find the village
cannot.sentence: “We cannot find the village,” the boy said.

took.contextMeaning: The traveler got a slow breath
took.sentence: “We only wanted to see the lanterns.” The traveler took a slow breath and spoke kindly.

slow.sentence: “We only wanted to see the lanterns.” The traveler took a slow breath and spoke kindly.

breath.simpleMeaning: Air in and out of your mouth
breath.contextMeaning: He breathed in and out slowly
breath.sentence: “We only wanted to see the lanterns.” The traveler took a slow breath and spoke kindly.

kindly.contextMeaning: He spoke in a nice, gentle way
kindly.sentence: “We only wanted to see the lanterns.” The traveler took a slow breath and spoke kindly.

okay.contextMeaning: They said it was all right

seemed.contextMeaning: The wind looked like it was going the right way
seemed.sentence: The wind seemed to move in the right direction, down toward the river path.

move.simpleMeaning: Go from one place to another
move.contextMeaning: The wind went toward the river path
move.sentence: The wind seemed to move in the right direction, down toward the river path.

right.sentence: The wind seemed to move in the right direction, down toward the river path.

direction.contextMeaning: The way the wind is moving
direction.sentence: The wind seemed to move in the right direction, down toward the river path.

down.contextMeaning: Toward a lower place near the river
down.sentence: The wind seemed to move in the right direction, down toward the river path.

group.contextMeaning: All walking together as one team
group.sentence: They walked as a group: the traveler, the two children, and the silver fox in front.

front.contextMeaning: The place ahead of the others
front.sentence: They walked as a group: the traveler, the two children, and the silver fox in front.

passed.contextMeaning: Went by the bridge and hill
passed.sentence: They passed the dark bridge and a small hill with flowers.

flowers.contextMeaning: Pretty plants growing on the hill
flowers.sentence: They passed the dark bridge and a small hill with flowers.

step.simpleMeaning: One small move with your foot
step.contextMeaning: One small move at a time
step.sentence: Step by step, the trees became fewer, and the warm lights of the village appeared again.

became.sentence: Step by step, the trees became fewer, and the warm lights of the village appeared again.

fewer.contextMeaning: Not as many trees as before
fewer.sentence: Step by step, the trees became fewer, and the warm lights of the village appeared again.

waved.contextMeaning: Moved their hands to say hello
waved.sentence: They waved at the houses and started to laugh with relief.

started.sentence: They waved at the houses and started to laugh with relief.

laugh.contextMeaning: Make happy sounds because they felt safe
laugh.sentence: They waved at the houses and started to laugh with relief.

relief.contextMeaning: Feeling happy because the hard part was over
relief.sentence: They waved at the houses and started to laugh with relief.

reached.sentence: When they reached the bridge, the traveler went straight to the broken lantern.

straight.contextMeaning: Went directly to the broken lantern
straight.sentence: When they reached the bridge, the traveler went straight to the broken lantern.

placed.contextMeaning: Put the stones back inside the lanterns
placed.sentence: They placed the three stones back inside the lanterns on the bridge.

puffs.sentence: Then, with three small puffs of wind, it touched each lantern.

touched.sentence: Then, with three small puffs of wind, it touched each lantern.

each.sentence: Then, with three small puffs of wind, it touched each lantern.

woke.contextMeaning: Started to shine and work again
woke.sentence: One by one, the lanterns woke up.

spread.simpleMeaning: Moved out over a wide place
spread.sentence: Soft yellow light spread across the stone bridge.

below.sentence: The river below looked bright, like moving silver.

soon.sentence: Soon, people began to arrive for the festival.

arrive.sentence: Soon, people began to arrive for the festival.

drums.simpleMeaning: Big things you hit to make music
drums.contextMeaning: Music instruments started making loud beats
drums.sentence: Drums started to play in the village square.

square.simpleMeaning: An open place in a town
square.contextMeaning: The open place in the village
square.sentence: Drums started to play in the village square.

carried.contextMeaning: Took food and tea with them
carried.sentence: Families walked together, and some people carried food and tea.

food.contextMeaning: Things families carried to eat later
food.sentence: Families walked together, and some people carried food and tea.

tea.simpleMeaning: A hot drink made with water and leaves
tea.contextMeaning: A hot drink people carried with them
tea.sentence: Families walked together, and some people carried food and tea.

rose.contextMeaning: Went up into the evening sky
rose.sentence: Then sky lanterns rose into the evening air.

floated.contextMeaning: Moved up gently in the air
floated.sentence: They floated up slowly, like warm little moons.

slowly.sentence: They floated up slowly, like warm little moons.

moons.simpleMeaning: Round lights in the night sky
moons.sentence: They floated up slowly, like warm little moons.

watched.sentence: The traveler watched, feeling proud and thankful.

proud.contextMeaning: Feeling happy because something good happened
proud.sentence: The traveler watched, feeling proud and thankful.

thankful.contextMeaning: Feeling glad for the good help
thankful.sentence: The traveler watched, feeling proud and thankful.

continue.sentence: And the village festival could continue without fear.

fear.sentence: And the village festival could continue without fear.

matters.contextMeaning: Is important because the story teaches something
matters.sentence: This story matters because small help can bring big light.

bring.sentence: This story matters because small help can bring big light.

big.sentence: This story matters because small help can bring big light.

feels.sentence: When one lantern is dark, a bridge feels unsafe.

unsafe.contextMeaning: Not good to cross because it may be dangerous
unsafe.sentence: When one lantern is dark, a bridge feels unsafe.

kind.contextMeaning: Nice and caring to other people
kind.sentence: But one kind person, one helpful animal, and one good choice can change the whole night.

person.sentence: But one kind person, one helpful animal, and one good choice can change the whole night.

helpful.sentence: But one kind person, one helpful animal, and one good choice can change the whole night.

animal.simpleMeaning: A living thing like a dog or cat
animal.contextMeaning: A living creature, not a person
animal.sentence: But one kind person, one helpful animal, and one good choice can change the whole night.

choice.contextMeaning: One thing you decide to do
choice.sentence: But one kind person, one helpful animal, and one good choice can change the whole night.

change.sentence: But one kind person, one helpful animal, and one good choice can change the whole night.

whole.sentence: But one kind person, one helpful animal, and one good choice can change the whole night.

learned.sentence: Today we learned a simple idea: a small action can help many people.

simple.sentence: Today we learned a simple idea: a small action can help many people.

idea.contextMeaning: A thought about how things work
idea.sentence: Today we learned a simple idea: a small action can help many people.

action.sentence: Today we learned a simple idea: a small action can help many people.

helped.sentence: The traveler listened, stayed calm, and helped step by step.

guided.sentence: The wind fox guided the way, and the lost children got home safely.

lost.simpleMeaning: Not able to find the way
lost.contextMeaning: The children did not know how to get home
lost.sentence: The wind fox guided the way, and the lost children got home safely.

home.contextMeaning: The place the children wanted to reach
home.sentence: The wind fox guided the way, and the lost children got home safely.

thank.simpleMeaning: Say you are happy and grateful
thank.contextMeaning: A polite way to show gratitude
thank.sentence: Thank you for listening and practicing your English with me.

listening.simpleMeaning: Using your ears to hear carefully
listening.contextMeaning: Paying attention to the words being said
listening.sentence: Thank you for listening and practicing your English with me.

practicing.simpleMeaning: Doing something again to get better
practicing.sentence: Thank you for listening and practicing your English with me.

lessons.contextMeaning: Simple learning pages or teaching parts
lessons.sentence: For more simple stories and lessons, visit ovi english dot com.

visit.simpleMeaning: Go to see a place or person
visit.sentence: For more simple stories and lessons, visit ovi english dot com.

dot.contextMeaning: The small mark in a website address
dot.sentence: For more simple stories and lessons, visit ovi english dot com.

com.simpleMeaning: A part of a website name
com.contextMeaning: The ending part of the website address
com.sentence: For more simple stories and lessons, visit ovi english dot com.

keep.simpleMeaning: Do it again and do not stop
keep.sentence: Keep listening, keep learning, and keep going.

learning.simpleMeaning: Getting new things in your head
learning.sentence: Keep listening, keep learning, and keep going.

going.simpleMeaning: Moving or continuing to do something
going.contextMeaning: Continue forward and do not stop
going.sentence: Keep listening, keep learning, and keep going.

opening.contextMeaning: The first part of the story or video

news.simpleMeaning: New information about something that happened
news.contextMeaning: A story about something that happened
news.sentence: [NEWS STORY] Early in the evening, a young traveler arrived at a quiet hill village.

practice.simpleMeaning: Do something again to get better
practice.contextMeaning: A question for learning and trying
practice.sentence: [PRACTICE] QUESTION: Where did the traveler arrive?

question.contextMeaning: A line asking the traveler to tell something
question.sentence: [PRACTICE] QUESTION: Where did the traveler arrive?

answer.simpleMeaning: What you say back to a question
answer.contextMeaning: The line that tells where the traveler arrived
answer.sentence: ANSWER: The traveler arrived at a hill village.

what.simpleMeaning: Asks about a thing or action
what.contextMeaning: Asks about the thing the traveler heard
what.sentence: QUESTION: What did the traveler hear near the bridge?

who.simpleMeaning: Asks about a person or animal
who.contextMeaning: Asks which person or creature the traveler met
who.sentence: QUESTION: Who did the traveler meet beside the broken lantern?

meet.simpleMeaning: To see and talk to someone
meet.contextMeaning: To come across someone beside the broken lantern
meet.sentence: QUESTION: Who did the traveler meet beside the broken lantern?

met.contextMeaning: Saw a small silver wind fox
met.sentence: ANSWER: The traveler met a small silver wind fox.

searched.contextMeaning: Looked in many places for something
searched.sentence: ANSWER: They searched on a forest path, near a stream, and in grass by the water.

why.contextMeaning: Asks for the reason the village felt happy
why.sentence: QUESTION: Why did the village become happy at the end?

become.simpleMeaning: To change and start being something
become.contextMeaning: To change into a happy village
become.sentence: QUESTION: Why did the village become happy at the end?

happy.contextMeaning: Feeling glad because things ended well
happy.sentence: QUESTION: Why did the village become happy at the end?

end.contextMeaning: The last part of the story
end.sentence: QUESTION: Why did the village become happy at the end?

begin.sentence: ANSWER: The bridge lights came back, the children were safe, and the festival could begin.

closing.simpleMeaning: The last part at the end
closing.contextMeaning: The last part that teaches a small idea
closing.sentence: [CLOSING] Today we learned a simple idea: a small action can help many people.
